content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
• 428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Robert Rath 
3550 Riverview Terrace 
Needles, CA 92363 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-16-162 
Dear Mr. Rath: 
September 6, 2016 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict of interest provisions of 
the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 
Please note that we arc only providing advice under the conflict of interest provisions of the 
Act and not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common law conflict of 
interest or Section 1090. 
QUESTION 
Do you have a conflict of interest in the decision to change the zoning of real property 
located approximately one mile from your real property interest and business interest? 
CONCLUSION 
No. You do not have a conflict of interest in this zoning decision because there is no 
reasonably foreseeable financial effect on your financial interests. 
FACTS 
You are a member of the City of Needles Planning Commission. Needles is adjacent to the 
Colorado River, at the California/Arizona border. You own a 50% interest in a mixed-use property, 
zoned Commercial, Residential, Resort ("CRR"), located along the Colorado River. This property 
includes your residence, an additional home, and a 10-space storage building of which you are the 
sole owner. You rent out the storage spaces, and currently store seven boats. You indicated in a 
follow-up telephone conversation that this business is a sole proprietorship. 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 ofTitle 2 of the California Code of Regulations, All 
regulatory references ;ire to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated, 


File No. A-16-162 
Page No. 2 
Approximately one mile away from your property are several parcels currently zoned CRR. 
These properties are located on the Needles Highway, near the Interstate 95 interchange. The 
property owner is seeking a zoning change to Highway Commercial ("C3") from the Planning 
Commission, to make the properties attractive to potential buyers. The parcels on the opposite side 
of the highway from these properties are zoned C3. One of the CRR properties contains a boat and 
RV storage business. It is a large operation which can accommodate approximately 400 boats and, 
unlike your business, has staff responsible for moving the boats in or out of storage. The other 
properties being considered as a part of the zoning change application are undeveloped. 
The City of Needles Zoning Map includes detailed definitions of the two zones at issue. 
The CRR zone is intended to allow a "full range of residential and commercial" uses, 
including "apartments, hotels, motels, restaurants, boat clubs, marinas, specialty and gift shops and 
convenience services, goods and supplies for boat owners, water skiers, scuba divers and the 
visiting public, using marina and aquatic recreational facilities." 
The C3 zone is "intended for uses that do not fit into any of the other commercial 
developments" and includes uses such as "shopping centers, automobile sales and services, 
commercial recreation, gasoline service stations, restaurants and motels and miscellaneous 
commercial uses." The definition also states that "many of the highway commercial uses would be 
located adjacent to freeway interchanges." 
As you noted in your request, a boat and RV storage business can operate in either the CRR 
or C3 zones, so a zoning change would not affect the existing boat and RV storage businesses, and 
would not affect the potential development of the other parcels as boat and RV storage facilities. 
There are currently several other boat and RV storage facilities located in Needles. 
ANALYSIS 
Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or using 
his or her position to influence a governmental decision in which the official has a financial interest. 
(Section 87103.) Section 87103 provides that a public official has a "financial interest" in a 
governmental decision if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial 
effect on one or more of the public official's interests. Interests from which a conflict of interest 
may arise are defined in Section 87103 and include the following: 
• An interest in a business entity in which the official has a direct or indirect investment of 
$2,000 or more (Section 87103(a)); or in which the official is a director, officer, partner, 
trustee, employee, or holds any position of management (Section 87103( d)). 
• An interest in real property in which the official has a direct or indirect interest of $2,000 or 
more. (Section 87103(b).) 
• An interest in a source of income to the official including promised income, which 
aggregates to $500 or more within 12 months prior to the decision. (Section 87103(c).) 


File No. A-16-162 
Page No. 3 
• An interest in a source of gifts to the official if the gifts aggregate to $460 or more within 12 
months prior to the decision. (Section 87103(e).) 
• An interest in the official's personal finances, including those of the official's immediate 
family. This is known as the "personal financial effects" rule. (Section 87103.)2 
Fi11a11cial 111/erest 
According to your facts you have a financial interest in your storage business as an 
investment and source of income. (Section 87103(a) and (c).) In addition, you have a financial 
interest in the real property, in which you have an interest of$2,000 or more.3 (Section 87103(b).) 
F oreseeabi Ii ty 
The standard for foreseeability differs depending on whether an interest is explicitly 
involved in the decisions at issue. (Regulation 1870 I.) A financial effect is presumed to be 
reasonably foreseeable when it is explicitly involved in a decision. Financial ·interests that are 
explicitly involved include an interest that is a named party in, or subject of, a government decision. 
(Regulation 1870l(a).) 
Neither your real property nor storage business interests are explicitly involved in the 
decision concerning the zoning change. For interests that are not explicitly involved, as here, a 
financial effect need not be likely to be considered reasonably foreseeable. Regulation 18701 (b) 
states, "In general, if the financial effect can be recognized as a realistic possibility and more than 
hypothetical or theoretical, it is reasonably foreseeable." Relevant examples of facts that may be 
considered in detennining whether a financial effect is reasonably foreseeable include: 
(2) Whether the public official should anticipate a financial effect on his or her 
financial interest as a potential outcome under nonnal circumstances when using 
appropriate due diligence and care. 
(3) Whether the public official has a financial interest that is of the type that 
would typically be affected by the tenns of the governmental decision or whether 
the governmental decision is of the type that would be expected to have a 
financial effect on businesses and individuals similarly situated to those 
businesses and individuals in which the public official has a financial interest. 
1 With respect to personal finances, Regulatton 18701.5(c) provides: ••If the governmental decision affects a 
business entity or real property in which the official has a financial interest, this regulation does not apply and 
materiality is determined under Regulation 18702.1 or 18702.2. Thus, we do not further analyze personal financial 
effects. 
3 You also have an interest in the customers of your business as sources of income because you wholly own 
your business. (Sections 82030 and 87103(c).) You did not ask about sources of income and thus, we do not analyze 
this financial interest in the letcer. 
.. 


File No. A-16-162 
Page No. 4 
(6) Whether the public official has the type of financial interest that would cause 
a similarly situated person to weigh the advantages and disadvantages of the 
governmental decision on his or her financial interest in formulating a position. 
(Regulation 1870 I (b ). ) 
Materiality 
The standard to determine materiality, for a business entity and business entity as source of 
income, not explicitly involved in the decision, is set forth in Regulation 18702.1 (b). Materiality is 
found if "a prudent person with sufficient information would find it is reasonably foreseeable that 
the decision's financial effect would contribute to a change . .. in the value of a privately-held 
business entity." (Regulation 18702.1 (b).) Relevant examples of decisions include those that would: 
( 1) Authorize, prohibit, regulate or otherwise establish conditions for an activity 
in which the business entity is engaged; 
(2) Increase or decrease the amount of competition in the field in which the 
business entity is engaged; 
(3) Increase or decrease the need for the products or services that the business 
entity supplies; 
(Regulation 18702.1 (b).) 
For real property not explicitly involved in a decision and pertinent to your facts, a 
foreseeable effect is material if the decision: 
( I 0) Would change the character of the parcel of real property by substantially 
altering traffic levels or intensity of use, including parking, of property 
surrounding the official's real property parcel, the view, privacy, noise levels, or 
air quality, including odors, or any other factors that would affect the market 
value of the real property parcel in which the official has a financial interest; 
(12) Would cause a reasonably prudent person, using due care and consideration 
under the circumstances, to believe that the governmental decision was of such a 
nature that its reasonably foreseeable effect would influence the market value of 
the official's property. 
Business Investment; Source of Income 
You stated that the properties in question are or can already operate as storage businesses, 
and may do so in either the C3 or CRR zone, as both zones permit commercial uses such as boat 


File No. A-16-162 
Page No. 5 
and RV storage. While the development of competing storage uses on the undeveloped property 
may have a financial effect on your business, that competing use is currently allowed and that 
would not change. Based on these facts, it is not reasonably foreseeable that the zoning change 
would foreseeably affect your business. 
Interest in Real Property 
Your real property interest is approximately one mile away from the properties subject to 
the zoning change. The facts do not indicate that there would be a foreseeable effect on your 
property interest as a potential outcome under nonnal circumstances because of the nature of the 
poposed change. In addition, as your property is approximately one mile away from the properties 
subject to the zoning change, this change would not have a substantial effect on intensity of use, 
including parking, of property surrounding your real property parcel, or on the view, privacy, or 
noise levels, or influence the market value of your property. (Re!,rulation 18702.2(a)( I 0) and ( 12).) 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
ZWN:jgl 
Sincerely, 
Hyla P. Wagner 
General Counsel 
By: 
Zachary W. Norton 
Senior Counsel, Legal Division

sections.question:
Do you have a conflict of interest in the decision to change the zoning of real property 
located approximately one mile from your real property interest and business interest?
sections.conclusion:
No. You do not have a conflict of interest in this zoning decision because there is no 
reasonably foreseeable financial effect on your financial interests.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Do you have a conflict of interest in the decision to change the zoning of real property 
located approximately one mile from your real property interest and business interest?

CONCLUSION: No. You do not have a conflict of interest in this zoning decision because there is no 
reasonably foreseeable financial effect on your financial interests.
